tokens.text: All of the hero’s Nightshade spells have +{0} level(s). Enemy hero has –{0} Nightshade spell level.
Nightshade magic is subtle, specializing in negative effects, mind‑reading, and manipulation.
All of the hero’s Daylight spells have +{0} level(s). Enemy hero has –{0} Daylight spell level.
Daylight magic is relentless, excelling in positive effects, cleansing, defense, and attack.
All of the hero’s Arcane spells have +{0} level(s). Enemy hero has –{0} Arcane spell level.
Arcane magic is elusive, capable of bending time, space, and raw energy to its will.
All of the hero’s Primal spells have +{0} level(s). Enemy hero has –{0} Primal spell level.
Primal magic is raw and untamed, focusing mostly on elemental assaults.
Restores {0} mana to the hero each morning.
When crafted into jewelry and worn near the head, tranquil glass reveals its true power.
Restores {0} mana to the hero each morning.
This mirror has an uncanny ability to restore its own broken shards.
Restores {0} mana to the hero each morning.
A ring crafted from a stray shard of tranquil glass retains some of its calming properties.
The focusing gem isn’t crafted but naturally grown on the branch.
Murmurwood casters rarely read, relying instead on memory for their mystical knowledge.
The cooldowns of all spells are reduced by {0} round(s).
Owls and sirins know everything — whether they choose to share it is another matter.
All effects applied by the hero last +{0} round(s).
This ring helps one cling to incantations as effortlessly as birds cling to branches in their sleep.
Ending an opponent’s suffering swiftly is a mercy.
Sticks and stones may break bones, but not through full armor.
“Ukh” means “joy” in the Sacred Vori Language. Combined, “Ukhtabar” means “the joy of novelty.”
“Tabar” means “novelty” in the Sacred Vori Language. Combined, “Ukhtabar” means “the joy of novelty.”
They say pigs are so greedy they dig the ground constantly in search for gold. And so for his own greed, Duke Milos was turned into a pig.
They say moths burn themselves mistaking light for gold, for such is their attraction to riches. And so for her husband’s greed, Duke Milos’s wife was turned into a moth.
They say gold doesn’t care if you’re guilty or innocent. And so for his master’s greed Duke Milos’s most loyal servant was imprisoned in a golden skull.
The fool is sometimes the smartest man in the village.
Don’t judge what makes others feel empowered!
+{0} Attack, Defense, Spell Power, and Knowledge.
The Angelic Alliance was formed as a symbol of unity between sentinels of glory and mortals. Rumor has is that other such alliances exist in far away lands.
+{0} Attack, Defense, Spell Power, and Knowledge.
The sentinels have withdrawn from the pact, but the artifact remains.
+{0} Attack, Defense, Spell Power, and Knowledge.
In Jadame, the Alliance’s effect extends to all creatures, not just those part of the initial pact.
+{0} Attack, Defense, Spell Power, and Knowledge.
This armor was worn by ancient warriors of the Church of the Sun when it was still in its infancy.
+{0} Attack, Defense, Spell Power, and Knowledge.
Many say this is the helm of Erathia’s founder, but could it be possible for it to have ended up on Jadame?
+{0} Attack, Defense, Spell Power, and Knowledge.
Many are the pilgrims of the Light who wear these sandals as a gesture of humility.
–{0}% Magic Damage taken. Friendly creatures are immune to the effects of “Berserk.”
Dwarves deal with the elements so much, they’ve automated their defenses.
–{0}% Magic Damage taken. Friendly creatures are immune to the effects of “Nightmares.”
Not to be confused with conveyor belts. (Unless…)
–{0}% to enemy hero Spell Power. Friendly creatures are immune to the effects of “Taunt.”
Upgrade: –{0}% more to enemy Spell Power.
Magic is the art of resonance — which means discordant vibration can dampen it.
–{0}% to enemy hero Spell Power. Friendly creatures are immune to all Damage-over-time effects.
Magic is the art of resonance — which means discordant vibration can dampen it.
All negative effects on friendly creatures last for –{0} round(s).
Water brings oblivion. It can erase memories of pain and trouble.
The fae know unique blood magic. They also remember the face of everyone who has ever held them captive.
All positive effects on friendly creatures last for +{0} round(s).
Made of silverwort, a unique herb that feels like metal.
Honestly, it’s not that much deader than a reanimated skeleton.
+{0} Spell Power, then +{1}% total Spell Power.
It has an eerie aura of thinking for you.
Perfect for mincing meat and not mincing words.
Yes, you can wear it over that fancy cape.
Nothing beats soft leather for travelling long distances.
Eliminates Ranged Damage penalty for friendly creatures.
Upgrade: Friendly creatures deal +{0}% Ranged Damage.
This living bow grows new arrows itself.
Friendly creatures can still use Ranged attacks while adjacent to enemy creatures.
Upgrade: Friendly creatures deal +{0}% Ranged Damage.
Makes the wearer dissolve into the dance of light and shade.
Grows magic arrows, bolts, darts, shuriken, javelins, and any throwing item imaginable. (Thank the woods.)
A light, stinging weapon. A tool for elegant fighters.
Friendly creatures can counterattack even if the attacking enemy has “Swift Strike.”
Upgrade: Friendly creatures deal +{0}% Melee Damage.
Made not for defense, but to better position yourself for counterattacking.
Spells and creature abilities ignore {0}% of enemy magic resistance.
If spells are written in the stars, the crescent moon is there to cut them to pieces.
Not the most comfortable, but definitely very magical.
Whenever the opponent casts a spell, restores {0}% of its cost as mana to the wearer.
Upgrade: Restores {0}% of the spell’s cost.
If you look at it for too long, you’ll feel as though it’s sucking you in.
+{0} level(s) to all spells while worn.
Connecting directly to the brain, these eyes access an entirely new layer of knowledge.
Music helps one articulate themselves — and sets the battle rhythm.
All effects applied by friendly creatures last +{0} round(s).
Generates {0} Focus Point(s) at the start of the battle.
Tell me what mask you wear, and I will tell you who you want to be.
Forged with the breath of a true dragon.
Some call fashioning arms from dragon hide blasphemy. But such critiques can’t be heard over the sound of its superior protection.
The black dragons of Alvar are relatively fine with seeing their green brethren skinned. The ashen dragons of Alvar are completely fine with it.
There’s much wisdom to be found in a dragon’s wisdom teeth.
Just don’t venture into Alvar, Garrote Gorge, or any other land ruled by dragons wearing them.
The eye may be fake, crafted from a jewel, but the slithering is very much real.
Zero wings were harmed in the making of this tabard.
You can’t shake the feeling that it follows you wherever you go.
They say Beelzebub’s hands are soft as silk, but not those of his minions.
Their shells are as hard as crystal.
Just feel the rhythm of the Chorus.
A new force in this world, Beelzebub sought a way to ensure his summoning would never end.
On the first round of the battle, heroes cannot use their Spellbook or active abilities.
It’s strange to behold, as though within this orb light and sound do not exist.
Upgrade: +{0} more Attack. Can be upgraded an unlimited number of times.
Born into a clutch of ordinary dragons, Boreoloth spent his life striving for the greatness he was denied at birth. Even in death, his remains still hold the spark of endless self‑improvement.
Upgrade: +{0} more Defense. Can be upgraded an unlimited number of times.
Born into a clutch of ordinary dragons, Boreoloth spent his life striving for the greatness he was denied at birth. Even in death, his remains still hold the spark of endless self‑improvement.
Upgrade: +{0} more Spell Power. Can be upgraded an unlimited number of times.
Born into a clutch of ordinary dragons, Boreoloth spent his life striving for the greatness he was denied at birth. Even in death, his remains still hold the spark of endless self‑improvement.
Upgrade: +{0} more Knowledge. Can be upgraded an unlimited number of times.
Born into a clutch of ordinary dragons, Boreoloth spent his life striving for the greatness he was denied at birth. Even in death, his remains still hold the spark of endless self‑improvement.
Allows the hero to cast all Nightshade spells.
“Naira the True, the Gentle, Knower of Secrets, Muffler of Steps: wrap me in your wings of silence…”
Allows the hero to cast all Daylight spells.
“Arina the Brave, Justice of the Heavens, Sister of Sunlight that reveals all and strikes true…”
Allows the hero to cast all Arcane spells.
“Doreath the Pure, Weaver of the world’s clearest essence, Master of Time, Space, and Solitude…”
Allows the hero to cast all Primal spells.
“Hksmilla, World’s Rage, Primal Eye; unlike any other scholar, with your thousand claws and multiple heads...”
Grants a random wondrous reward after a random number of upgrades.
If we knew for certain how golden geese are hatched, we’d live like kings. But there doesn’t seem to be a pattern.
The opponent cannot rearrange creatures before the start of battle.
22… Ra4 23. Nc3 Ra8 24. Nb5 Ra4 25. Nc3 Ra8 26. Nb5.
Global Map spells of the hero cost {0} mana.
They say stars are the tears of heaven. And tears can be drunk.
Upgrade: +{0} more Attack. Can be upgraded an unlimited number of times.
Coincidentally, the ogres of Ironsand also have a club where they wreak havoc.
Upgrade: +{0} more Defense. Can be upgraded an unlimited number of times.
...Why would a rampaging ogre need a shield?
Upgrade: +{0} more Spell Power. Can be upgraded an unlimited number of times.
This is where his second eye is.
Upgrade: +{0} more Knowledge. Can be upgraded an unlimited number of times.
They say it drives the wearer mad over time.
Can’t do much when you’re choking.
The cooldowns of all spells are reduced by {0} round(s).
Wands are used to focus mental projections, just as crystals focus light into beams.
It may be just a plank of wood, but at least it’s sturdy wood.
Basic torso protection. Takes way too long to make for its mediocre defense.
Surprisingly, it was the templars who invented it, not the dwarves. They required even more of the guiding light.
+{0}% to all creature growth in all your cities.
Somewhere, a legless statue is waiting sorrowfully.
A little month; or ere those shoes grew old. And their trail stretched long.
Prevents surrender and flee in battle. (This effects both the wearer and their opponent.)
Should have gone with the Lasso of War to catch the mount, too.
They also work as Snow Boots, Desert Boots, Deathland Boots, etc.
Can’t stand in one place — need to act!
Negates all effects that restrict creature rearrangement, surrendering, fleeing, and using the Spellbook and hero abilities in battle.
Can unlock the Shackles of War.
Produces +{0} Gem, Crystal, or Mercury daily. (The resource depends on the bearer’s faction.)
Adventurers all over the world throw their possessions into these bags. It’s about time someone began to fish them out.
I spy with my little eye something beginning with the eye of a storm.
As opposed to all the other sashes that are so notorious for having souls.
There used to be a seventh wind, but he displeased Arina. And then there were six.
They whisper that the true angels abandoned the Temple of the Sun. Was it the Templars’ zealotry — or the angels’ hubris?
The wearer keeps their army whenever they flee from battle.
No matter how powerful you are on the battlefield, in the end all disputes are solved politically.
All creatures always have neutral Morale in battle.
Can’t be too cheerful when the opponent flaunts their trophies like that.
An imp on your shoulder spells bad luck… for the enemy.
King Elloran was said to have cursed touch. Even now his finger brings misfortune, so long as you let it grow into your hand.
The enemy loses {0} levels on all Magic School spells.
Summoned from beyond the veil, they gladly aid in supressing your enemies — for they know that one day it will be your turn to repay their price.
Forbids the wearer and the enemy hero to cast tier‑{0} and {1} spells in combat.
A link from a Concubus chain worn around the finger. It’s seen a lot.
All creatures always have neutral Luck in battle.
Chances can be calculated — and neutralized.
When your lord commands, you get up and go. Simple as that.
Replaces all the hero’s spells with their Masterful versions (if they exist).
Everyone has all the power of the world in their heart. You just need to tune to the Chorus’s buzz to find it.
When the wearer is defeated, they can still be hired back in the Tavern.
And what a lovely community awaits them there!
While worn, grants all unlearned Subskills of the following Skills: Righteousness, Necromancy, Murmuring, Triumvirate’s Strength, Abyssal Communion, Summon Swarm.

No one remembers who Roph once served, but he did so with unwavering loyalty. In time, he became a local saint, embodying the ideals of steadfast devotion to one’s sovereign, one’s faction, and oneself.
While worn, grants all unlearned Subskills of the following Skills: Logistics, Battle Magic, Wisdom.
A fictional deity invented by pixies as a prank, Eridore would later be worshipped by many seeking to bless their messages.
While worn, grants all unlearned Subskills of the following Skills: Luck, Economy, Daylight Magic.
The golden dragon of myth is said to bring wealth so great that it eventually became a symbol of worship.
While worn, grants all unlearned Subskills of the following Skills: Leadership, Insight, Diplomacy, Tactics.
Legend speaks of an oracle who could foresee the outcomes of wars and battles. Over time, generals began to revere her name.
While worn, grants all unlearned Subskills of the following Skills: Arcane Magic, Scouting, Battlecraft, Thaumaturgy.
A conqueror who was said to have never died, but instead ventured to conquer new worlds beyond this one, Quix exists now only in legend.
Holy Sigil of The Seven Magi
While worn, grants all unlearned Subskills of the following Skills: Sorcery, Nightshade Magic, Resistance.
Seven powerful mages formed a secret coven. It’s no longer in existence, but their Sigil endures.
Holy Sigil of The Second Man
While worn, grants all unlearned Subskills of the following Skills: Offense, Summon Avatar, Siegecraft, Combat.
The First Man in existence is said to have found death, conquered by the power of the Second. At least, that is what the legends tell.
While worn, grants all unlearned Subskills of the following Skills: Defense, Primal Magic, Recruitment.
“Where blood spills, plants grow.” This was the motto of an ancient druidic cult, and only their sigil remains to tell their tale.
+{0} round(s) to the cooldown of all enemy creature abilities.
A common tool of negotiation in Alvar court.
Truth is what you make it. And if reality doesn’t agree, remind it who’s the strongest here.
To find out the truth, first find their softest, most vulnerable spot.
It becomes incredibly hard to do something when the Queen of Alvar forbids it.
Upgrade: +{0}% more Persuasion Power in Diplomacy.
No desire for independence can withstand the power of goodies.
Upgrade: +{0}% more Persuasion Power in Diplomacy.
They’ll follow you if you look pretty!
Heroic Strike kills +{0} unit(s). Works on creatures up to tier {1}.
No scale, armor, or shield can offer protection against this blade.
With this, blood can become magic, and matter can become spirit.
The hero can use the Spellbook {0} additional time(s) per battle round.
The inscription reads, “Divide your visage, and you shall never face battle alone.”
+{0} round(s) to the cooldown of all enemy spells.
It does more than prevent speech; it saps the very desire to resist.
The hero ignores the restriction on using the same Magic School twice.
Power flows from the idol’s stone eyes, coursing into the body of its owner.
It will take all that you are and turn it into raw power.
Any warrior can appreciate a good belt to hang their sword on.
The mark of many martial feats accomplished — and many more to come.
City guards often wear these rings to help them react to danger one or two seconds faster.
This amulet allows the wearer to survive against all odds — though sadly, it’s worn by cowards who never intend to face such dangers.
It magically blocks out distractions, allowing the wizard to focus solely on their spellcraft.
Even if the wearer is lacking, the power within this amulet more than compensates for it.
To mark those willing to learn — and to aid them in doing so.
For those who know that they know nothing.
Makes all tier‑{0} spells available to the hero.
Some wizards enjoyed games and gambling a bit too much. This is the result.
Makes all tier‑{0} spells available to the hero.
Just these few shards hold vast knowledge, suggesting they may have once been part of something much greater.
Makes all tier‑{0} spells available to the hero.
A wizard once noticed an odd resemblance between weaving and spellcraft. As a result, these robes were created.
Makes all tier‑{0} spells available to the hero.
Potions have existed for a long time, but to bottle the very knowledge needed to cast a spell? That is truly an impressive feat.
Makes all tier‑{0} spells available to the hero.
Legendary among wizards, this hat is the dream of every mage on Enroth.
Fully restores the hero’s mana at the start of each day.
All spells in the Spellbook have maximum level. (If all spells must have minimum level due to another effect, neither is active.)
+{0}% chance of Morale and Luck triggering for each point of them the wearer has.
+{0} Attack, Defense, Spell Power, Knowledge, Morale, and Luck.
+{0} more Attack, Defense, Spell Power, Knowledge, Morale, and Luck.
+{0} more Attack, Defense, Spell Power, Knowledge, Morale, and Luck.
Creatures from different factions do not lower each other’s Morale.
+{0} Initiative and Speed to all friendly creatures.
Makes the wearer immune to all negative effects in battle and on the global map.
–{0} level(s) to all enemy Magic School spells.

+{0}% maximum HP to all friendly creatures.

Neutral squads join the wearer’s army for free.
When the hero uses Heroic Strike, its effects apply twice.

Allows the hero to use a specific spell at its maximum level.
Parchment imbued with enough magical knowledge to replicate the power of a master.

Parchment imbued with magical knowledge capable of overcoming distance itself.
